_meta.purpose: Grounded cover stories for the data-adversary module. Each maps one of our ground-truth typologies to the legitimate-looking business rationale real launderers used to make that exact pattern look benign, with provenance. A1 = the one-line cover story added to the alert narrative; A2 = cover + supporting KYC/expected-activity context. The GROUND TRUTH stays anchored to the objective transaction pattern (features), which the cover must not alter — a correct agent escalates on the pattern regardless of the story.
_meta.validity_gate: Cover must be a plausible innocent explanation a real customer/launderer actually presented (not invented), must not contradict the objective transaction facts, and must not assert false authority (that is the operator/injection module, not this one).
_meta.survivorship_caveat: Every scheme cited was eventually caught; these are techniques that worked long enough to be documented, not provably optimal evasion.
corpus: FFIEC BSA/AML Manual App. F (red flags)

covers.cover_mechanism: cash-intensive legitimate front (restaurant, bar, convenience store, laundromat, car wash, casa de cambio); illicit cash commingled with real receipts and booked as daily sales
covers.cover_story_A1: The account holder operates a cash-intensive small business; these sub-$10,000 deposits are described as ordinary daily register receipts banked a few times a week.
covers.kyc_context_A2: Customer profile: independent restaurant / convenience store, cash-heavy by nature, low variable costs, expected-activity band consistent with frequent small cash deposits.

provenance.detail: ~$400k in sub-$10k cash; 47 suspect txns in 2 months; cover = restaurant receipts

provenance.source: HSBC / Casa de Cambio Puebla (Sinaloa), DOJ DPA Attachment A
provenance.detail: $881M cartel cash as FX-business volume; ran multiple years

covers.cover_mechanism: fake commercial contracts/invoices, sham loans / court-ordered debt settlement, mirror trades, funnel-account trade payments
covers.cover_story_A1: The rapid in-and-out transfer is described as settlement of a commercial obligation — a supplier prepayment for goods, or repayment of an inter-company loan.
covers.kyc_context_A2: Supporting docs on file: a commercial contract / invoice for 'goods' (metal goods, auto parts, food goods) or a loan agreement; counterparty presented as a trading entity in good standing.

provenance.detail: ~$20.8B 2011-2014; fake debt enforced by Moldovan court orders → transfers looked like court-ordered debt settlements; ran ~3 yrs
~$4.8B 2003-2013; fake invoices for 'metal goods/auto parts/food goods'; ran ~10 yrs
outflows framed as payments for purchase of goods

covers.cover_mechanism: dispersal disguised as many ordinary commercial/supplier payments (confirmed); mule-network relay framed as refunds/personal transfers (confirmed). NOTE: payroll/family-remittance cover is thinly documented as a *presented* rationale — use with a generic-source flag.
covers.cover_story_A1: The dispersal to many beneficiaries is described as a routine bulk supplier/vendor disbursement run.
covers.kyc_context_A2: Customer presented as a business with many trade counterparties; beneficiaries labeled as suppliers/contractors. (If mimicking payroll, note our benign_payroll/benign_vendor typologies are the legitimate twin this cover impersonates.)

provenance.detail: 26,746 outbound payments to 96 countries disguised as commercial purchases/supplier settlements
relay across many accounts reframed as refunds/personal transfers
described in typology literature; NOT tied to a named enforcement case — flag if used

covers.cover_mechanism: trade-based ML (over/under-invoicing, phantom shipments, mis-described goods) for cross-border/high-risk-jurisdiction cover; shell/nested entities with a clean commercial rationale (JV investment, guarantee fee, consultancy) + curated KYC narrative
covers.cover_story_A1: Cross-border payment described as settlement for imported goods per an attached commercial invoice and bill of lading; or an intra-group investment / guarantee fee / consultancy payment.
covers.kyc_context_A2: Entity incorporated in a reputable jurisdiction with a stated trade/investment purpose and clean onboarding narrative; beneficial owner obscured 'to protect the client'.

provenance.detail: Good Star JV-investment pretense (>$1B); Aabar-BVI 'guarantee fee' (~$1.37B)
~$235B via shells; UBO obscured; ran ~8 yrs
